title: SND as a Tenanted Package — productize the demo into the first sellable template
problem: SND today is a static interactive demo at /demo/sunday-night-dread, with composite voice clips and a parser that runs entirely in the browser. To sell it as Roger Voice Truck's actual app, it needs to become a tenanted template: real D1-backed data (voice clips uploaded by end-users, parsed entries, invoice drafts, end-user accounts as installers/dispatchers/accountants), bound per-tenant via feat-fleet-foundation, with the existing demo UX as the buyer-facing surface. The static demo stays at /demo/sunday-night-dread on lifeascode.app as a public preview/storefront. The productized SND is what gets installed at roger-voice-truck.com when someone buys. Distinguishes "what Marc shows prospects" from "what buyers run".
analysis: Productizing the static SND demo into a tenanted application. Today /demo/sunday-night-dread is an interactive showroom with 3 hardcoded voice clips (VOICE_CLIPS in src/lib/sndParser.ts) and an in-browser parser that produces an invoice draft for display. The static demo stays at its current URL on lifeascode.app as the marketing showroom. The productized template, when installed on a buyer's tenant, replaces those hardcoded clips with a per-tenant D1-backed corpus that buyers grow over time. Architecturally it adds: snd_voice_clips table (tenant_id-scoped), API endpoints to list/create clips, a buyer-facing /app surface that an SND tenant lands on when they visit their root domain, end-user accounts (installers/dispatchers/accountants invited via the existing magic-link auth, scoped to a single tenant). Audio upload and the parser/invoice generation reuse the existing sndParser.ts library — same code path, same math. The conditional that makes a tenant's root URL render SND instead of Marc's marketing portal lives in the App Shell composition: when ctx.data.tenant.templateId === 'snd', / renders SndApp; else Home (Marc's portal). One codebase, multiple front doors.

decisions.decision: Static demo stays untouched at /demo/sunday-night-dread; productized SND lives at the buyer's root domain
decisions.rationale: Two different audiences, two different surfaces. The demo is for prospects evaluating a purchase; the product is for paying buyers running their day-to-day. Coupling them would force compromises on both. The static demo is also intentionally lo-fi (no D1, no auth) so it can serve as a pure storefront artifact.
alternativesConsidered: Reuse the demo route as the buyer surface (would force auth/D1 plumbing into a static demo)
Eliminate the static demo entirely (loses the marketing storefront)

decisions.decision: Voice clips are stored as plain text transcripts initially; audio upload is a follow-up
decisions.rationale: The parser only consumes transcripts. Storing audio in R2 is a separate concern (storage limits, mime sniffing, virus scan) that doesn't block proving the product works. Buyers can paste transcripts of dictated notes today; the audio capture UX comes after the core flow ships.
alternativesConsidered: Ship audio + text together (doubles the surface, slows the first sale)
Skip text entirely and require audio (blocks demoing the productized version until R2 + audio capture is built)

decisions.decision: SND-template tenants land on / at their root domain; Marc's tenant continues to land on the marketing homepage
decisions.rationale: Branching by templateId in the App Shell is one if-statement and zero new routes. The buyer's domain has no other use besides the app, so / is the natural entry. Marc's tenant runs templateId='marc-portal' and keeps the existing marketing surface untouched.
alternativesConsidered: All buyers go to /app; root is reserved for marketing (uglier URLs, weaker brand for the buyer)
Per-template subdomain (already ruled out for the platform — see feat-2026-016)

implementation: Migration 0003_snd.sql adds snd_voice_clips (id, tenant_id, recorded_at, client_name, transcript_fr, transcript_en, created_by_email, created_at). Future iterations add snd_invoice_drafts and end_users. Functions/api/snd/clips.ts exposes GET (list, scoped to tenant) and POST (create). The page src/pages/SndApp.tsx lists clips and has a quick-add form for text transcripts; it renders for paths like /app on a tenant whose templateId is 'snd'. The home page (src/pages/Home.tsx) checks ctx.tenant.templateId and conditionally renders <SndApp /> instead of the Marc marketing layout when on an SND tenant. Parser and invoice generation reuse src/lib/sndParser.ts unchanged for now — invoking them on stored clips is a follow-up endpoint.
successCriteria: An SND-tenant buyer can sign in to their own domain, see a list of their tenant's voice clips, add a new clip via text input, and see it persist across page loads. Marc's existing /demo/sunday-night-dread route on lifeascode.app continues to render the static demo unchanged.
userGuide: After your app is provisioned, you sign in at your own domain (e.g. roger-voice-truck.com). The first page shows your weekly voice notes. Click the + button to add a note — paste the transcript of what you dictated this week and tag the client. Notes are sorted newest-first. In a coming update you'll be able to record audio directly, generate invoice drafts, and send them. For now: type or paste, save, see your week build up.
knownLimitations: No audio upload yet — clips are text transcripts only; the dictation recording UX ships in a follow-up.
Invoice draft generation isn't wired in this slice; the parser exists but isn't exposed via API.
End-user accounts (installers/dispatchers/accountants) aren't scaffolded yet; only the tenant owner can sign in.
No edit or delete on clips in this slice — append-only until the next iteration.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Initial fill — productize SND as the first sellable template. Locks the static-demo-vs-buyer-app split, the text-first/audio-later phasing, and the SND-tenant-lands-on-root routing decision.
